title: corydalis cytisiflora subsp. altecristata
intro: Corydalis cytisiflora subsp. altecristata, also known as the "Alpine Corydalis," is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, native to high-altitude mountain regions. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as corydaline and tetrahydropalmatine. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved cardiovascular health. Corydalis cytisiflora subsp. altecristata is often prepared for medicinal use in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific study.
